title: Graduate Service Desk Engineer
description: **About Ekco** Founded in 2016, Ekco has quickly become one of Europe’s fastest\-growing cloud solution providers and your trusted security\-first Managed Service Provider. IT leaders choose Ekco to drive operational efficiency, scale smarter and stay ahead of risk – powered by local expertise, delivered at European scale. We specialise in helping organisations advance their cloud maturity guiding transformation, strengthening security, and maximising the value of their technology investments. ️ In simple terms: we help organisations modernise with confidence securing their systems, optimising their cloud, and keeping them resilient in a rapidly changing world. Today, we’re a thriving team of 1,000\+ talented and supportive colleagues across the UK, Ireland, Benelux, South Africa, and Malaysia—and we’re continuing to grow. At Ekco, how we work matters as much as what we deliver. Our people live by four core values that shape everything we do: * **On It**: We take ownership, follow through, and get things done. * **All** In : We collaborate, support each other, and commit fully to shared goals. * **Connected**: We build trusted relationships with colleagues, clients, and partners. * **Hungry to Grow**: We stay curious, keep learning, and push ourselves to the next level. If these values resonate with you, you’ll feel right at home here. **The Role** We are seeking a Service Desk Engineer to join our growing Group IT team. Ekco is an MSP providing technical services to clients. Group IT provides technical services to Ekco itself. You will join a team of seven to support Ekco’s operations in the UK, and across the wider global business. This is a hybrid role with a successful candidate stationed between our UK offices in Milton Keynes and Chancery Lane (London) at least three days a week. You’ll need to live in commuting distance of at least one of those offices, with the periodic travel to the other office covered by Ekco. There may be occasional travel opportunities to our other offices in Ireland/Netherlands. As a junior role we do not expect many years of experience, but do expect an eagerness to learn, a passion for IT and all things tech! **Day\-to\-day at Ekco:** * Managing your tickets with high quality triaging, notes, communication, and customer service. * Coordinating with HR to assist joiners and leavers in the business. * Provide hardware break\-fix support. * Laptop \+ phone imaging, and device inventory management. * Coordinating with the logistics team on deliveries of hardware * Work with the rest of the Group IT team to provide remote technical support for users across all our regions. * Assist with the technical maintenance of our UK offices. * Coordinate with other technical teams and third\-party vendors. * Document changes and processes in our documentation solution when required. **What we are looking for:** * Passion for IT and Technology * Experience supporting hardware and software, at home, school or in the workplace. * Confident using Windows and/or macOS * Basic understanding of Microsoft Azure, Intune and Windows Server * Demonstrable experience meeting deadlines * Able to explain basic IT Concepts such as Servers, Clients, DNS, DHCP, Firewalls, switches and routers. * Ability to learn and research new concepts and technologies independently. * Excellent English professional communication skills, both verbal and written. * Team player. **Desirable:** * 1\+ years of technical experience in a similar role * Any work experience in a professional role * Confident using Linux and other technologies * Basic administration experience of Microsoft 365, Intune, Windows Desktop/Server, Active Directory macOS * Scripting and or programming experience * Industry certifications – Azure, Microsoft 365\. A\+ and Network \+ desirable * Degree in relevant field * Experience with ticketing systems and/or the ITIL Framework **Benefits / Perks** * ️ Time Off: 25 days annual leave \+ public holidays * Birthday Leave: One extra day off to celebrate • Company Pension Scheme • ️Health Care * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for wellbeing support * ️ EkcOlympics: Global team activity challenges * Unlimited access to Pluralsight for continuous development * Real opportunities to grow, including international progression **Why Ekco** * ️ Microsoft’s 2023 Rising Star Security Partner of the Year * First Irish Microsoft MSP to achieve all four Microsoft Security Specializations * Ranked 4th fastest\-growing technology company in the Deloitte Fast50 Awards * A culture rooted in diversity, equality, inclusion \& belonging * A commitment to internal mobility and career progression * Flexible, family\-friendly working at the heart of our culture * Proud to be your trusted security\-first Managed Service Provider chosen by IT leaders to drive operational efficiency, scale smarter and stay ahead of risk.

title: Growth Analyst
description: We are looking for a Growth Analyst to join us in our mission to grow our understanding of marketing performance \& effectiveness across Flutter UKI. You will be part of the team responsible for guiding Flutter’s marketing investments through insightful business intelligence. Reporting into the Senior Growth Manager for UKI, you will play a central role in supporting the customer growth strategy for one of our core brands. You will deliver high\-quality insight, reporting and analysis across acquisition, retention and monetisation, helping the business understand customer behaviours, performance drivers and opportunities to unlock value. This is a high\-impact role for an analytically driven individual who brings a commercial focus to their work and is motivated by transforming data \& insight into clear, actionable recommendations that support business growth. **Introducing the team** This opportunity offers a great opportunity to join a recently established team that gets to work alongside the teams that power some of the biggest brands in the UK. The UKI Marketing BI team are part of the wider centralised BI function which covers many disciplines beyond Marketing analytics, including Research, Commercial, Product and Safer Gambling. We work closely with stakeholders across the business, providing timely and valuable insight whilst making recommendations to be actioned by the teams. **What You'll Do** * Producing insight and reporting for one of our UKI brands (Paddy Power, Betfair \& Sky Betting \& Gaming), delivering clear analysis across acquisition, retention, reactivation and customer value * Monitoring key marketing performance metrics and identifying trends, changes or risks in customer behaviour * Delivering deep insight into customer behaviour across the lifecycle, identifying growth drivers, value shifts and opportunities to unlock incremental performance * Supporting the Senior Growth Manager in deep\-dive investigations into customer behaviour, growth drivers and growth opportunities * Collaborating with Marketing BI colleagues to design \& develop a comprehensive marketing self\-serve capability, reducing manual reporting across Digital Marketing channel teams * Contributing towards reallocation recommendations by highlighting high\-value opportunities and areas of inefficiency * Working closely with Marketing, Finance \& Commercial teams to understand brand strategy and ensure alignment on growth opportunities * Educating your Flutter colleagues to improve our understanding of marketing effectiveness as a business **Skills, Capabilities \& Experience Required** * Strong experience in using SQL to manipulate data \& deliver analytical solutions within a business context. * Experience with visualisation tools (e.g Looker, PowerBI) desirable. * Skilled at condensing analysis into actionable insights to inform business decisions within marketing and influence business performance. * Solid foundational knowledge of marketing covering both offline and online channels and an understanding of CRM activities and processes. * Understanding of key marketing analytics concepts including experimentation, attribution models and key data sources to support analysis (e.g. GA4, Appsflyer). * Ability to present confidently \& effectively. * Ability to build strong and effective relationships both within the Analytics team and across the business at all levels. * Highly organised, with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage competing priorities. * You will be self\-motivated, and action orientated, able to take a lead and drive action and change where required. **About Flutter UK \& Ireland** Our division operates four of the most popular and trusted brands in the market: Sky Betting and Gaming, tombola, Betfair and Paddy Power. Together the Flutter UK\&I brands offer market leading entertainment to millions of customers every single week! Over the last couple of decades, they have all ridden the wave of digital betting with industry firsts like ‘cash\-out’, ‘Request a Bet’, betting exchanges and free to play games like Soccer Saturday Super 6\. **Working here** Do you want to work somewhere extraordinary? From the people you spend your days with, to the ground\-breaking projects, no two days will be the same. With a philosophy of ‘Together We Are More’ our 7,000\+ colleagues come together to form an expert community across technology, product, commercial, data, infrastructure, marketing, and a myriad of subject areas. We listen without judgement, encourage \& support, and help build others up. Working at Flutter UK\&I means you can be yourself, work how and where suits you best \-and let your personality shine! **We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.** **If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.**

title: Hotel Management Graduate Programme 2026
description: NYX Hotels is giving laidback luxury: we are THE destination! Our NYX team (we call them City Lovers!) is a diverse bunch of vibrant people, who are on a mission to make NYX the first\-choice for guests looking for a high\-end stay with a unique, urban feel. NYX Hotels is a part of the Leonardo Hotels portfolio. **Please note: Successful applicants will be considered for opportunities across multiple Leonardo Hotels locations in the UK and Ireland.** Just about to graduate or graduated last year and wondering what’s next? With over 57 hotels in the UK \& Ireland and over 300 worldwide, and award\-winning L\&D offerings, we have exciting opportunities to not only take part in our graduate programme but also grow within an ever\-expanding company to achieve your career goals with the support of our L\&D programmes. **The Graduate Programme** * 18\-month Management Development Programme * The programme is a challenging mix of work experience, off the job learning and self\-learning * Exposed to as much of the hotel operation as possible through departmental rotation and relationship with Deputy General Manager or Operations Manager * Early focus on the Duty Manager role and responsibilities within the first 3\-6 months * Fixed term contract with the aim of securing a permanent management role by the end of the programme. **What we are looking for?** * A passion for genuine hospitality * A proactive, positive and resilient attitude * A degree in any of the following disciplines; Hotel/Leisure/Tourism or International Hospitality * Ideally, you will have previously worked in an operational role in Hotels, Leisure or Tourism * Flexibility on placement location to get the best out of your programme **What’s in it for you?** * 18\-month Management Development Programme * Extensive personal and professional development * Support of industry professionals with a dedicated mentor along with a programme sponsor. **What's next?** * Apply online here today! * Applications close July 2026 * We will be in touch by August 2026 * You will take part in a 2\-step process – 1 assessment centre and if successful, a final interview with the General Manager of your placement hotel * Opportunities to start with us from August/September with the graduate programme commencing November 2026 Some of the perks our Hotel Management Graduate Programme 2026 could enjoy include:* Special rates on Leonardo Hotel rooms across the UK \& Europe * Company\-wide recognition scheme: you could earn vouchers to spend on a wide range of high street shops * Talent referral scheme * Thank You Week: from ice cream trucks to yoga classes and lots in between! * Wellbeing Calendar * Ongoing job\-related training programmes with clear paths for progression We’re committed to creating a workplace where every individual—regardless of background, identity, or lived experience—is welcomed, valued, and respected for who they are. Belonging means embracing our differences and ensuring everyone feels seen, heard, and empowered. Why come join us as a Hotel Management Graduate Programme 2026? At NYX Hotels, we look after our City Lovers just as well as we look after our guests. Once you’re part of the NYX team, you’re part of the Leonardo Hotels community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland. Our regular Learning Bites, “Come Join Us Guest Care” training and Wellbeing webinars all help your progress with us. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: Immigration Consultant
description: **Job Description** **Immigration Consultant (Cork)** **Contract** : Full\-time, permanent **Team** : Ireland Inbound **Office Location** : Cork, Ireland **The Role:** The Immigration Consultant will be responsible for managing the day\-to\-day casework of various Irish immigration matters, from case assessment to reviewing and filing applications, as well as providing advice to clients Immigration Case Work * Assess client objectives (typically multinational companies and high\-net\-worth individuals), providing guidance to achieve objectives and making compliance and regulatory risk assessments. * Conduct in\-depth assessment of immigration compliance pathways, including data collection, verification, and risk analysis for individual cases or larger projects. * Troubleshoot cases and establish strategy with the Manager, ensuring work is reviewed and signed off at key benchmark stages. * Prepare and draft employment permit applications, entry visas, and a range of immigration applications for Fragomen's multinational clients under Manager supervision. * Develop strategy for graduate Irish placements, complex EU Directive implementation, nationality and citizenship matters, and broader compliance work, providing advice to businesses, individuals, and families. Client Support * Provide training and immigration consultancy to clients, preparing presentations in advance to support process improvement and regulatory compliance. * Act as a key point of contact for advice on large\-scale business projects, global mobility regulatory and compliance requirements, and general project management. * Provide process overviews, regulatory updates, and contribute to strategy discussions with Global Mobility and HR teams; occasional onsite client visits may be required. * Liaise with government agencies such as DETE, ISD, and Irish Embassies abroad. * Determine parameters for internal data collection, interpret reports, and recommend solutions and proposals to clients. * Ensure all correspondence is clear, concise, and appropriate for the audience, maintaining client style. * Take ownership of client reporting. Team Support * Implement in\-house procedures to improve efficiency using Fragomen’s internal systems, and work with client accounts to ensure effective strategies, project management, and regulatory compliance. * Delegate work using software management tools. * Analyse case management data to identify best practices, address software pitfalls, and improve protocols and workflows. * Assist with office\-wide projects on efficiency, business development, and maintenance of the regulatory knowledge database. **What we are looking for:** The ideal person for this role will be a self\-starter with a background in a busy client facing role with excellent organisational skills. Also required: * Bachelor’s degree * Professional experience with immigration provider preferred * Client\-facing experience * Understanding of immigration compliance, regulatory and Right to Work Requirements * Strong interest in immigration as well as the desire to further career within this field * Knowledge of case and data management and risk analysis * Understanding of billing and billing procedures * Ability to work effectively with clients and cross\-functional teams * Strong ability to prioritize workload, manage competing deadlines, and consistently meet service level agreements * Excellent attention to detail and ability to multi\-task in a fast\-paced environment * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to work effectively with clients, internal teams, and external stakeholders. **What to expect:** * Once you have submitted your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your skills and experience. If these match the requirements of the position, a member of our team will contact you to schedule an initial phone call. * If shortlisted, you will be invited to participate in our interview process, which will consist of at least two stages, one of which will be conducted in\-person. Depending on the role, you may also be required to complete a written assessment. * After the interviews, we will carefully evaluate all candidates and make our final decision. You will be notified of the outcome and feedback will be provided. * At Fragomen, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible workplace for all applicants and employees. If you require any specific accommodations or support during the interview process, please let us know, and we will consider what reasonable adjustments are possible to meet your needs **Who we are:** At Fragomen, we are more than a global immigration services provider – we are pioneers shaping the future of mobility. With a passion for navigating the complexities of immigration, we help individuals and businesses transcend borders, unlocking opportunities around the world. As a forward\-thinking, global business, we combine deep expertise with innovative technology to craft solutions that not only simplify the immigration process but also drive strategic success for our clients. Whether it is assisting multinational companies or helping people start new chapters in their lives, we are driven by the belief that global movement is the key to unlocking a world of possibilities. We are proud of our team spirit, and we like to reflect this in the way that we work and everything we do. Our Responsible Business Practices ("RBP") prioritize Fragomen's social responsibility through a cohesive strategy covering five key focus areas: Corporate Social Responsibility, Culture and Inclusion, Sustainability, Well\-Being and Pro Bono. Our RBP initiatives offer great opportunities for everyone at Fragomen to feel included, get involved and shape thoughtful initiatives and activities. For more information, please visit our website – https://www.fragomen.com/about/responsible\-business\-practices.html . *Fragomen is committed to promoting equal opportunities for all employees and applicants, regardless of race, ethnicity, heritage, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or intersex status.* *All offers and/or employment contracts are contingent upon the successful completion of the Firm’s pre\-employment screening process. This process may include verifying the candidate’s identity, confirming legal authorization to work in the offered position’s location, and conducting a comprehensive background check, where permitted by local regulations. We use limited AI‑assisted tools for administrative screening purposes only \- never for decision‑making. All hiring decisions are made by people. Applicants may have rights to information and explanations regarding the use of such tools, or request human review, as required by applicable regional laws.*

title: Industrial/Window Cleaning Operatives
description: Agua\-Clean Services is a professional industrial cleaning contractor specialising in commercial window cleaning and high\-level access services throughout Ireland. We deliver reliable, safety\-driven, and environmentally responsible cleaning solutions to clients across the commercial, industrial, and retail sectors. Due to continued growth, we are seeking motivated and reliable Industrial Window Cleaning Operatives to join our team. **Key Responsibilities:** * Carry out professional window cleaning and industrial cleaning services at client sites across Dublin and nationwide. * Work safely at height, including use of MEWPs (where certified). * Operate both independently and as part of a skilled, safety\-focused team. * Maintain excellent on\-site communication and customer service standards. * Comply with all company health, safety, and environmental procedures. * Adapt to flexible working hours, including early mornings, evenings, weekends, and overtime as required. **Candidate Requirements:** * Full\-time availability with flexibility for evenings, weekends, and overtime. * Strong punctuality, reliability, and readiness for early starts. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Professional, friendly attitude with strong customer service skills. * Safe Pass and Manual Handling certification preferred (training can be arranged). * Non\-EEA candidates must hold a valid full\-time work permit for Ireland. * Full Category B Driving Licence desirable (driving roles available for eligible candidates). Previous commercial window cleaning experience is an advantage but not essential, as full training will be provided. **What We Offer:** * **Day rates from €134–€167**, depending on experience and duties performed.. * Regular overtime opportunities. * Comprehensive training and structured upskilling pathways. * Career progression opportunities, including training toward Rope Access roles. * Company vehicle available for eligible candidates. * Benefits package including: * Bike to Work Scheme * Employee Assistance Programme * Sick Pay Scheme Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€43,464\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Investment & Development Analyst
description: **Analyst – Industrial \& Logistics \- Investment \& Development** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Salary:** €55,000 \- €65,000 per annum \+ bonus \+ benefits **About the Company** A leading pan\-European real estate investment and asset management platform focused exclusively on urban and last\-mile industrial \& logistics assets. Founded in 2016, the firm manages €4bn\+ of assets across key European gateway cities, executing a research\-led, data\-driven investment strategy targeting supply\-constrained, high\-growth markets. With fully integrated capabilities across investment, development, asset management, and leasing, they are at the forefront of delivering best\-in\-class logistics real estate solutions aligned to structural e\-commerce growth and urban supply chain evolution. **The Opportunity** Seeking a highly analytical and driven Investment Analyst to join its growing team in Dublin, with a focus on development\-led opportunities within the industrial \& logistics sector. This role offers direct exposure to the full investment lifecycle, including origination, underwriting, development analysis, and execution across European logistics markets. The successful candidate will work closely with senior investment professionals and play a key role in supporting the expansion of the development platform. **Key Responsibilities** **Investment \& Development Analysis** * Build and maintain complex, institutional\-grade financial models for acquisitions and development projects (including land, speculative and pre\-let schemes) * Conduct detailed cash flow modelling, IRR/NPV analysis, and sensitivity scenarios * Analyse development appraisals including construction costs, phasing, leasing assumptions, and exit strategies * Support underwriting of value\-add and development\-led logistics investments **Deal Execution** * Assist in the execution of transactions, including due diligence, investment memoranda, and IC materials * Coordinate with internal teams (development, asset management, finance) and external advisors * Review legal, technical, and commercial documentation **Market Research \& Strategy** * Undertake granular market analysis across Irish and European logistics markets * Track supply\-demand dynamics, rental growth, land values, and occupier trends * Support identification of off\-market and thematic investment opportunities **Portfolio \& Asset Support** * Monitor performance of development and stabilised assets * Support business plan execution and value creation initiatives **Candidate Profile** **Experience** * 1–3 years’ experience within: * Real estate private equity / investment management * Development advisory or principal development * Investment banking (real estate, infrastructure, or M\&A) * Direct exposure to real estate financial modelling is essential * Experience in industrial/logistics or development strongly preferred **Technical Skills** * Advanced financial modelling skills * Strong proficiency in Excel and real estate modelling frameworks * Ability to analyse complex capital structures and development scenarios **Academic Credentials** * Strong academic background (e.g. top\-tier university) * Degree in Real Estate, Finance, Economics, Engineering or related discipline * Progress toward or completion of CFA / ACA / MSc Real Estate / Finance highly desirable **Personal Attributes** * Highly analytical with strong attention to detail * Commercial mindset with genuine interest in logistics real estate and development * Proactive, entrepreneurial, and capable of operating in a lean, high\-performance team * Strong communication skills and ability to engage with senior stakeholders **Why Join?** * Exposure to a market\-leading, specialist logistics platform with strong institutional backing * Opportunity to work on high\-conviction, development\-led strategies in Europe’s most supply\-constrained urban markets * Collaborative, entrepreneurial culture with direct access to senior decision\-makers * Accelerated career progression within a fast\-growing, high\-performing investment platform

title: Investment Analyst - Global Fund
description: **Job Title: Investment Analyst** **Sector: Global Investment Firm** **Location: Dublin \- Hybrid** **Salary: Base negotiable plus exceptional bonus opportunity and benefits** **Our Client** Our client are a Global Investment Firm. They have a track\-record of delivering exceptional results and have an excellent reputation globally. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity for a driven and ambitious individual who is seeking to advance their career in an exciting industry. You will be part of an investment team that has exposure to all projects, large scale, niche, and highly specialised ventures. The role will potentially enable the successful candidate to put themselves on a path to a seat at the top table. **Who should apply?** Candidates with prior experience in finance with the following: * 2\-5 years’ experience in accounting, investment banking or structured finance role * 3rd level degree in accounting, economics, finance, actuary, or another numeric discipline * Excellent academics and proven track\-record of high achievement * Ability to analyse large data sets * Highly motivated to take on responsibilities * Post\-grad qualification in finance advantageous but not essential * Concise reporting writing ability * High level of proficiency in financial modelling * Strong attention to detail in both written and numerical data/documentation * Keen interest in financial markets **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting directly to the Investment Manager works closely with other members of the Investment team, duties will include: * Financial modelling * Co\-ordinating due diligence and carrying out detailed market research * Creating and writing key reports for internal and external stakeholders * Assisting members of the wider team with ad\-hoc presentations and analysis * Reviewing equity assumptions * Stress testing and sensitivity analysis for all potential investments **Interested in this position?** To apply, please submit your CV to Aonghus Cody who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact Aonghus directly at 01 529 4200\.

title: Investment Operations Associate
description: Our client, a expanding Asset Manager based in Dublin City centre, is seeking a investment operations professional to join their team. This role provides excellent exposure to a asset management environment and strong career progression opportunities. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in the investment management industry **Key Responsibilities** * Support portfolio managers by implementing trades and monitoring investment positions * Deliver regular insights on fund performance, positioning, and key metrics * Assist in launching new funds and maintaining smooth day\-to\-day operations * Collaborate across teams to prepare materials for internal governance and decision\-making * Enhance processes and provide data support for client and sales\-related requests **Key Requirements** * 2\+ years experince in investment management/funds * Ability to work efficiently between different internal business units * Knowledge of derivatives * Strong analytical skills **How to Apply** For further information on this opportunity, please apply via the link with your CV. Due to application volume, only candidates who meet the criteria outlined above will be contacted.

title: Junior QS
description: *Please note: This job requires you to have Irish/UK experience and can not offer any sponsorship* **Job Summary:** Our client is offering an excellent opportunity for a Quantity Surveyor to join a growing business with a clear strategic plan for expansion. Reporting to the Commercial Director and based in East Meath on a hybrid basis, this role will suit someone looking to progress their career in a company with strong long\-term ambition. **Role Description:** * Manage all aspects of quantity surveying for construction projects. * Develop the supply chain. * Oversee project procurement. * Prepare monthly project payment applications and cost reports. * Monitor project costs and agree variations. * Remeasure site works. * Report monthly to senior management on financial performance. * Prepare and present cost and risk analysis. * Manage all aspects of tendering, including financial assessments. * Ensure best value is achieved across all elements. * Audit and report on subcontractor and supplier returns. **Suitable Candidates Should Have:** * Residential experience, with modular or timber frame experience preferred. * A minimum of 2 years’ experience in a similar role. * A third\-level qualification in Construction, Building, or a related field. * Strong communication skills and in\-depth knowledge of construction practices, trades, and techniques. * A proven track record in securing profitable projects. * Excellent IT skills. * A full clean European driving licence and access to transport. * Strong Microsoft Office skills. * The ability to work well as part of a team. * The ability to meet deadlines. * Excellent numeracy and computer skills. * A professional presence, dress, and demeanour. **Salary:** Negotiable, and will include a suitable benefits package.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor – Civic ITS (Ireland)
description: Civic ITS delivers the intelligent transport systems that keep Ireland’s roads safe, efficient, and moving. As our project portfolio grows, we’re looking for a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** who’s eager to learn, contribute, and build a long‑term career in a specialist engineering environment. This is an ideal role for someone with early QS experience — or a recent graduate — who wants hands‑on exposure to real infrastructure projects and strong mentorship from an experienced commercial team. **Role Overview** You’ll support the commercial and contractual delivery of ITS projects, working closely with senior QS staff, project managers, engineers, and suppliers. The role blends structured learning with real responsibility, giving you the foundation to grow into a fully autonomous QS within Civic. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in preparing **cost estimates, budgets, and financial reports** * Support the development of **BOQs, tender documents, and procurement packages** * Help manage **subcontractor quotations, comparisons, and contract administration** * Track **project progress**, gathering data for valuations and variations * Contribute to **payment applications** and commercial documentation * Maintain accurate records and support compliance with **Public Works Contracts** * Work with project teams to monitor **costs, risks, and commercial performance** * Build strong working relationships with suppliers, contractors, and internal teams **About You** * Degree (or final‑year student) in **Quantity Surveying** or a Construction related field * 1\+ years’ experience in QS, commercial, or construction roles * Interest in infrastructure, electrical, civil, or technology‑based projects * Strong numerical, analytical, and organisational skills * Willingness to learn, ask questions, and take ownership of tasks * Good communication skills and a collaborative mindset * Full clean driving licence (or working toward one) **What Civic ITS Offers** * Competitive salary with clear progression pathways * Mentorship from experienced QS and commercial professionals * Exposure to nationally significant ITS and smart mobility projects * A supportive, unified team culture with strong governance * Training, CPD support, and opportunities to specialise Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €35,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Application question(s): * Do you hold a Degree (or are final‑year student) in Quantity Surveying or a Construction related field? Experience: * QS, commercial, or construction: 1 year (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Junior privacy counsel
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * Partner with Product, Engineering, and Security teams to embed privacy\-by\-design principles and translate regulatory requirements into practical technical solutions. * Identify privacy risks and opportunities early, helping shape compliant product features, workflows, and internal processes. * Manage privacy\-related legal work—using AI tools to accelerate drafting and initial review of DPAs, privacy policies, LIAs, and DPIAs, while applying your judgment to validate, adapt, and ensure compliance with global data\-protection regulations. * Conduct privacy audits and coordinate work with external consultants and legal advisors, ensuring alignment across business and technical teams. * Monitor evolving privacy regulations and case law, sharing clear guidance internally to support ongoing compliance and sound decision\-making. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Loss Prevention Officer
description: TK Maxx At TJX Europe, every day brings new opportunities for growth, exploration, and achievement. You’ll be part of our vibrant team that embraces diversity, fosters collaboration, and prioritises your development. Whether you’re working in our Distribution Centers, Corporate Offices, or Retail Stores—TK Maxx \& Homesense, you’ll find abundant opportunities to learn, thrive, and make an impact. Come join our TJX team—a Fortune 100 company and the world’s leading off\-price retailer. **Job Description:** **Could you be one of our Loss Prevention Officers in TK Maxx Dundrum?** As the world's leading off\-price retailer of clothing and homeware, we're all about putting our customers first. With thousands of shops worldwide, we aim to provide a positive and memorable experience for all. Working as a **Loss Prevention Officer**, you'll help to deliver our goal of keeping our customers, associates and assets safe. Experience of being in a customer facing role, within a fast\-paced retail environment or security guard role is ideal but not essential. **Candidates are required to hold a valid Security Guard (Static) licence issued by the Private Security Authority.** **In this role:** You will be supporting a positive customer experience within our stores by being a visible, friendly, approachable presence at our entrance doors. You will be focused on deterring loss on the salesfloor whilst acting as an ambassador for the loss prevention function. You will work closely with the store team to maintain security of our product and together you will aim to deliver effective conflict resolution when required. You will need to demonstrate your ability to remain calm and professional under pressure. You will also be self\-motivated, resilient and driven to make a difference with a natural ability to communicate. **In return, you'll get an in\-depth programme of training when you start.** **Are we right for you?** We're all about helping you develop yourself. The skills you'll learn working within Loss Prevention will help you be at your best. Plus, you'll be surrounded by the support of our fun and caring team. Also as a TJX Europe associate, you'll enjoy such things as: Competitive Salary Uniform Provided Comprehensive package of benefits to support your mental, physical and financial wellbeing such as access to an employee assistance program, associate discount and service awards Join us, and you’ll be a part of our thriving, growing European business filled with opportunities for growth and recognition. We’re proud of our culture, we work hard, have lots of fun, and give back to many causes we care about. It will be a role you won't just like but love. As proud as we are of our past success, it’s our future that excites us most. We strive to provide opportunities for growth, recognition and a competitive salary and benefits package. Share our determination to think bolder and bigger, and be part of our future. We consider all applicants for employment without regard to age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnerships, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief and/or sexual orientation.**Address:** Unit 3\.4**Location:** EUR TK Maxx IE Store 436 \- Dundrum

title: Mainframe Engineer
description: Are you a Softwar looking for your next big opportunity contracting in Ireland? Then I have the job for you! **About Your New Employer** * One of the biggest privately owned investment banks in the world. * Be part of one of the key teams for the business globally. * You will be focused on delivering cutting\-edge retirement application software. **About Your New Job as a Mainframe Engineer** * You will be a key individual contributor on an agile team delivering high\-quality mainframe solutions for financial services. * You will design, develop, and maintain software using mainframe technologies such as COBOL, DB2, CICS, and related tools. * You will participate in the full software development lifecycle, including architecture and application design, deployment, and automation. * Your role involves close collaboration with product owners, technical leads, and cross\-functional team members in an agile/SCRUM environment. * You will have the opportunity to guide, encourage, and motivate your fellow engineers, fostering a culture of engineering excellence. **What Skills You Need in this Job as a Mainframe Engineer** * Senior level experience with software design and development experience. * Essential expertise in Mainframe technologies (COBOL, DB2, CICS, COBOL\-stored procedures, NSP). * Experience developing and deploying batch and CICS applications. * Good working knowledge of mainframe tools (ENDEVOR, FILE\-AID, ABEND\-AID, CONTROL\-M). * Proven track record in product delivery within agile scrum development environments. * Strong analytical, communication, organisational skills, and ability to manage multiple tasks. * Financial services experience is a plus but not required. * Bonus knowledge: Java, web services (REST/SOAP), AWS, containerisation. **What’s on Offer in this job as a Mainframe Engineer** * Daily Rate up to €550/€600 * Hybrid working environment that is remote the majority of the month with one full week on\-site per month * Good likelihood of contract extensions **What’s Next** If you think this job is for you, apply now! Looking forward to talking to some great candidates!

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Support Engineer
description: **Maintenance Support Engineer – Syringe Filling Operations** **Position Summary** The Maintenance Department Support Engineer is responsible for day\-to\-day commercial support within the maintenance department. The role will span numerous areas such as, but not limited too, shutdown coordination, engineering reliability projects, supporting and leading all quality non\-conformances and leading maintenance equipment RCA’s, leading and coordinating all training\-related activities within the maintenance department of a pharmaceutical syringe filling plant. There will also be a training aspect of this role which ensures that maintenance personnel are effectively onboarded, trained, and continuously developed to support safe, compliant, and efficient manufacturing operations. The position also plays a key role in building and sustaining a high\-performing training hub aligned with site training academy initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** **1\. Support Maintenance Reliability and Shutdown Planning** * The **Maintenance support engineer** is responsible for improving the reliability, availability, and maintainability of plant equipment and utilities within the ADL manufacturing facility. The role focuses on leading and developing shutdown planning and sustaining a robust reliability framework that ensures right\-first\-time maintenance execution, minimises unplanned downtime, and optimises asset lifecycle performance in support of safe, compliant, and efficient operations. * The role partners closely with Maintenance, Engineering, Operations, and Quality to analyse equipment performance, identify failure modes, and implement preventive and predictive maintenance strategies. The Maintenance Reliability Engineer also leads continuous improvement initiatives to reduce non\-commercial time, improve equipment uptime, and increase overall plant availability. **2\. Hiring \& Onboarding Coordination (Maintenance Department)** * Coordinate all hiring logistics for maintenance technicians in partnership with HR and maintenance leadership. * Lead onboarding processes, ensuring a structured and compliant induction for all new hires. * Serve as the primary point of contact for new maintenance employees during onboarding. * Ensure all onboarding documentation, training assignments, and compliance requirements are completed on time. **3\. competency frameworks, training pathways, and certification requirements. * Monitor trainee progress and provide feedback to supervisors and stakeholders. * Identify skill gaps and implement targeted development plans for both new and existing technicians. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical or Chemical) * Manufacturing experience in biopharmaceutical Formulation/Fill/Finish or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) facilities * Solid leadership, technical writing, and communication/presentation skills * Strong understanding of GMP, regulatory requirements, and training compliance standards. * Proven experience in training program development, technical training, or learning \& development. * Experience working with maintenance teams, equipment, and troubleshooting processes is highly desirable. **Skills \& Competencies:** * Excellent strategic planning and demonstrated project management skills * Strong organizational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities. * Instructional design and training delivery expertise. * Analytical mindset with the ability to track and improve training effectiveness. * Leadership capability with a focus on coaching and developing others. * High attention to detail and commitment to quality and compliance. **Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)** * Time\-to\-competency for new maintenance hires * Training completion and compliance rates * Effectiveness of training programs (assessment results, performance improvements) * Reduction in maintenance\-related deviations linked to skill gaps * Continuous improvement of training materials and programs **Reporting Structure*** Reports to: Maintenance Manager / Site Training Lead (as applicable) * Key Interfaces: HR, Engineering, Operations, Quality, Training Academy

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Mechanical Design Engineer, Mercury Headquarters, Ballycoolin, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centers, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centers, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realize their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. The Design Engineer plays a key role within the pre‑construction team, supporting multiple enterprise and hyperscale data centre projects across a diverse client portfolio. Working at the front end of projects, the role focuses on early\-stage design development (PCSA), technical assurance, constructability, and risk mitigation in close collaboration with clients, consultants, and the wider supply chain. This position offers exposure to complex, high‑value data centre developments and the opportunity to influence design, cost, and delivery outcomes from inception. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** Provide support to Pre\-construction team on all Engineering functions including, but not limited to the following: * Familiarization of Design/ specifications and drawings to fully understand the scope of the Project. * Assist in generation and finalization of estimates, tender documents and resourcing of tender projects. * Review the MEP design and conduct value engineering exercises to optimize mechanical design solutions. * To support the engineering requirements through managing \& liaising with Key Project Stakeholders (Client, Suppliers, Sub\-contractors, Vendors). * Ensure the design process recognizes current legislation, standards and codes of practice, where appropriate. * Lead the complete design team including the design consultant, the client, architect and BIM team. * Interface management between all parties to allow for an efficient design process. * Responsible for management of RIBA design processes and procedures. * Review equipment specifications and generate procurement packages. * Assessment of Sub\-contract and Supplier Tender returns for Technical compliance with design intent specifications. * Review the construction methodology to identify program implications and building logistics. * Interrogate tender documentation (drawings, specifications, tender addenda, and all other documentation) for commercial risks and scope gaps relating to deliverables. * Identify project risks and mitigation plans to deal with these risks. * Mentor and support less experienced engineers, promoting knowledge sharing and professional development. * Evaluate and promote the use of new engineering practices and processes to enhance delivery time of projects. * Attend industry events and training sessions to maintain a current understanding of market trends and best practices. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * 8\+ years experience working with a recognized contractor as a mechanical engineer. * Relevant engineering experience in a general contracting environment is required. * Honors degree (NFQ Level 8 for Ireland) in Mechanical engineering. * Proven ability to lead projects, manage technical and financial aspects, and deliver high quality mechanical systems. * Design and implementation of mechanical systems such as Critical cooling, HVAC, plumbing, and fire protection. * Knowledge of electrical systems supporting the mechanical plant. * Strong understanding of industry standards, regulatory requirements, and value engineering principles. * Experience in mentoring junior engineers and fostering collaboration within multidisciplinary teams. * Competence in design software such as Navis works, ACC, BlueBeam or similar platforms. * Strong communication skills to liaise effectively with stakeholders and external consultants. **Desirable** * Chartered Engineer preferred (or working towards it). **Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.** Apply Now

title: Mechanical Design Engineer Apprentice
description: **Application Deadline:** 5 May 2026 **Department:** Design **Location:** Dundalk **Description** **Team Overview** The Design department is responsible for developing innovative, practical, and fully coordinated mechanical and electrical building services solutions tailored to each project. Working closely with clients, project teams, and the BIM department, the design team transforms concepts into detailed, build\-ready technical designs that meet regulatory standards, client specifications, and sustainability goals. Their work underpins the technical accuracy and efficiency of our installations, ensuring seamless integration from planning through to construction. **Role Overview** We are seeking a Mechanical Design Engineer Apprentice to provide practical, real\-world mechanical engineering experience within the rapidly growing alternative energy and low\-carbon heat sector. You will be supported by an established mechanical and electrical design team and will gain exposure to mechanical design, coordination, site activity and commissioning, helping you understand how projects move from concept through to operation. **Job Title** \| **Mechanical Design Engineer Apprentice** **Location** \| **Dundalk, Co. Louth Hours** \| **Monday\-Thursday 8am\-5pm and Friday 8am\-3pm** **Salary** \| **Competitive Depending on Experience** **Responsible to** \| **Building Services Manager** **What You'll be Doing** This role involves preparing comprehensive mechanical design proposals, including detailed installation drawings, plant selection, and technical schedules, alongside performing heating and hot water load calculations to ensure accurate system sizing. It includes developing project\-specific BMS control panel designs and operation descriptions, conducting site surveys of existing and new property services to identify suitable heating solutions, and producing reports based on findings. The position also requires attending design meetings, collaborating closely with contracts managers and commissioning teams to support system commissioning and optimize BMS performance, and working within an established design team using advanced AutoCAD and MEP 3D design software.

title: Nature & Land Use Expert
description: When you join KPMG Ireland, you are joining a community of over 4,200 employees who collectively work together, both in person and virtually, to achieve their full potential. We are known as an organisation who want you to come as you are and do work that truly matters. Joining KPMG Ireland is an opportunity to enhance your career, not only in the work that you enjoy doing, but equally through the global connections that you make, the lifelong learning that you can accomplish and the chance to make your mark. Are you our next **Nature \& Land Use Expert?** **Location: Dublin/Cork, as part of our hybrid working model.** **Grade: Associate Director** **Contract type: Perm** KPMG Sustainable Futures is seeking an experienced Associate Director to join our Nature \& Land Use unit. As a senior member of the team, you will lead and deliver client engagements focused on nature, biodiversity and land use across public and private sectors. You will also work with the wider team on adjacent projects on climate risk, sustainable finance, circular economy and transition planning (including responding to key drivers such as EU CSRD and TNFD). **A Snapshot Of Your Peer Group** * Bachelor’s degree required: Master’s preferred in nature, biodiversity, land use or a related discipline. * 8\+ years’ professional experience (including consulting) in nature and biodiversity, natural capital, ecosystem services or related fields. * Strong technical knowledge and hands on experience applying nature\-related standards and frameworks (e.g., CSRD ESRS, TNFD/LEAP, SBTN, SBTi) and natural capital concepts and standards (including natural capital accounting where relevant). * Strong understanding of Irish, EU and global sustainability regulation and market trends, and nature\-related disclosure requirements. * Proven experience delivering advisory engagements across public and private sector clients, translating complex scientific and regulatory topics into clear, actionable insights for senior audiences. * Excellent project management and delivery leadership, including oversight of quality, budgets and client relationships. * Commercial mindset with a track record contributing to business development and growth of client services. * Experience coaching and developing junior team members in a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment **Your responsibilities and achievements will evolve as you enhance your career with us. Here is what you can initially expect:** * Lead delivery of client workstreams end\-to\-end: project planning, analysis, synthesis of evidence, and quality assurance of outputs. * Produce high\-quality deliverables (reports, presentations and models), turning technical content into decision\-useful insights for senior stakeholders. * Design and facilitate client meetings and workshops, guiding stakeholders through findings, options and practical next steps. * Deliver work across nature, biodiversity and land use topics, including impacts/dependencies, risks/opportunities, targets, natural capital accounting, strategy and reporting/disclosure recommendations (TNFD/CSRD\-aligned where relevant). * Work closely with colleagues across climate risk, decarbonisation, sustainable finance, circular economy, ESG strategy, transition planning and reporting in KPMG Ireland and across our global network. * Help develop and apply KPMG tools, methodologies, propositions and thought leadership. * Support business development through proposals, client relationship building and market engagement. * Coach junior colleagues and contribute to the growth of the Sustainable Futures team and the Nature \& Land Use unit. **Your Team** KPMG Sustainable Futures is a cross\-functional team within KPMG Infrastructure Advisory, supporting corporate and public sector clients across decarbonisation, climate and nature transition planning, and the wider sustainability agenda. Learn more: https://kpmg.com/ie/en/home/services/sustainable\-futures.html This role reports directly to the Director of Nature \& Land Use. **Discover How KPMG Can Enrich Your Life Beyond Your Career** **Benefits Tailored For You** At KPMG, we understand that life is multifaceted. That’s why we offer a flexible benefits package designed to support your unique lifestyle. Our benefits include: * Discounted Health Insurance: Prioritise your well\-being with our health insurance options. * Additional Annual Leave: Purchase extra leave to spend more time with friends and family or to travel. * Enhanced Family Leave: Enjoy more time with your family with our generous leave options. * Emergency Childcare Cover: For times when your regular childcare plans fall through * Social Events: Connect and network with colleagues through our engaging social events. * Early Finish Fridays: Start your weekends early on bank holidays and during the summer. * Relocation Assistance: Smooth your transition if you’re moving to Ireland. * Learning \& Development: Access our extensive supports, including LinkedIn Learning, to grow your skills. We also offer a variety of flexible working options to help you balance your professional and personal life. **Career Progression with Continuous Support** From the moment you join KPMG, you’ll receive dedicated support from your performance manager to enhance your skills. Our award\-winning Business School provides continuous learning opportunities to deepen your expertise. We stand out as the only Big Four firm to announce promotions four times a year, offering you frequent opportunities for career advancement. **Commitment to Inclusion \& Diversity** Inclusion, diversity, and equity are at the heart of KPMG. We celebrate diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and we’re committed to fostering an inclusive and equitable environment at every level. Our goal is to attract and retain the best talent by building a culture where everyone can achieve their potential. Learn more about our commitment here \- https://kpmg.com/ie/en/home/about/inclusion\-diversity\-equity.html **Ready to Join Us?** If you meet most of the criteria, we encourage you to apply by submitting your CV. KPMG is an equal opportunities employer, welcoming applications from people of all backgrounds. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations during the recruitment process if needed. Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. For any specific queries about the role, please contact Niamh McRory. For a glimpse into life at KPMG, follow \#ExperienceKPMG on LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and X. **Please note that agency assistance is not required for this position.** By clicking apply to this role your application will not only be considered for this position, but your details will be stored on our database and may be considered for similar future positions that may arise. This means that the personal data contained in your application and provided to KPMG as part of the application process may be retained by us for up to 18 months from the date of your application and you hereby agree to that. If you do not wish your details to be stored on our recruitment database, please email careers@kpmg.ie stating this and we will ensure that such information is not stored other than for the purposes of this application.

title: Night Support Worker
description: **About the porject:** Long\-Term Accommodation services provide support for single people who are homeless with mental health, addiction or personal care needs. Residents are mostly people who have been homeless for long periods of time and who do not have the capacity to live independently. Every resident has their own room and there are also large communal rooms and gardens. The project operates a case management system where staff provide support to residents through individualised support plans. Residents are also taught life skills to increase their ability to maintain independent or semi\-independent living. **About the role:** Contract Type – Full Time Permanent. Working Hours – a 5 week rolling roster of night shifts, 37\.5 hours a week, from Monday to Sunday availability. Salary \- \- €33,500 \-38,250 depending on experience \- Plus 12% allowance per nights Location – Dublin City Centre All our emergency services are provided on a low threshold basis with a harm reduction model and a full suite of services for clients. The aim of our emergency services is to support our residents to move on to and to be able to sustain long\-term accommodation. We have several measures in place to ensure a quality, wraparound support service is provided to enable people to move out of homelessness. As a Support Worker, you’ll help to meet client needs on a nightly basis; you will provide in\-the\-moment support and safety to clients. You will contribute to ensuring effective and consistent best\-practice standards are followed in the Dublin Simon Community emergency accommodation services. The Support Worker role is an opportunity to gain frontline experience and many support workers graduate on to project worker roles within the organisation. As well as our induction program, you will be provided with 10 training days throughout the year, as well as on\-the\-job coaching and mentoring, ensuring you have the support and training to succeed in this role. **Your responsibilities** * Carry out health and safety checks as per local policy and recording of same. ▪ Provide support to the clients at night, recording on support plans, updating PASS and nights bed list, encouraging sleep hygiene. * Provide care interventions, including daily living skills support, relapse prevention support. * Ensure all new clients receive a robust induction to the service and are aware of their rights and responsibilities. * Ensure building is secure at night and that all systems are working (alarms, CCTV, emergency response details) and adhering to the lone working policy. * Therapeutic and diversionary interventions, providing emotional and motivational support, promoting positive mental health attitudes, self\-esteem and self\-respect. * Support the manager in the implementation of the strategic and operational action objectives. Taking responsibility for developing and implementing strategic and operational goals. * Preparations of rooms for service user intake to ensure standards are maintained. ▪ Crisis and conflict management. * Ensure consistent handovers, diary, incident reports and follow\-ups, in accordance with organisation policies. * Accommodation management; ensuring routine maintenance is carried out, cleaning, security inspections and bedroom health and safety inspections occur, in line with operational procedures and standards. * Ensure neighbourhood policy is implemented and managed at all times. * Active interventions; harm reduction, overdose prevention. * Interpersonal skills development, dealing with relationships, family liaison and behavioural management issues. **Essential for the role:** * Level 6, 7 or 8 in a relevant field with 6 months experience in similar setting (including voluntary work and college placement) Or * Level 5 in relevant field with 12 months experience in similar setting (including voluntary work and college placement) * A Passion for helping people. * Flexibility to work in an agile environment. * Willingness to learn. * Experience in carrying out Health \& Safety inspections and audits. **Benefits to working with Dublin Simon Community:** * 25 days annual leave. * 5% matched contribution to your pension. * Paid Sick Leave Policy. * Paid Maternity Leave Policy. * Bike to Work Scheme. * Commuter Travel Tax Savers Ticket. * 1st\-week Comprehensive Training. * Progression Opportunities. ​Shortlisting will be in progress throughout the period that the vacancy is live, so you may be called for interview and/or the vacancy may be filled before the closing date. Job Types: Permanent, Full\-time Pay: €33,500\.00\-€38,250\.00 per year Application question(s): * In order to work in Ireland a non\-EEA National, unless they are exempted, must hold a valid Employment Permit. Do you require an Employment Permit to work in Ireland? * Garda Vetting applies to roles with access to children or vulnerable persons. We will check with An Garda Síochána for any criminal record or relevant information. Have you ever been, or are you currently, subject to criminal charges or investigations? Work Location: In person

title: Operations Analyst, Mandiant Consulting, Pre- Sales Support
description: Note: Google's hybrid workplace includes remote and in\-office roles. By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **In\-office locations: Dublin, Ireland.** **Remote location(s): Ireland.** **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * 5 years of experience in an operations or customer support role with a professional services firm, software or technology\-based company. * 5 years of experience with Salesforce or related Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience in customer\-facing roles. * Experience with sensitive materials and handling confidential information. * Experience with working in Salesforce. * Excellent customer service and communication skills. **About the job** A problem isn’t truly solved until it’s solved for all. Our unique combination of renowned frontline experience responding to some of the most complex breaches, nation\-state grade threat intelligence, machine intelligence, and the industry's best security validation ensures that Mandiant knows more about today's advanced threats than anyone. **Responsibilities** * Support pre\-business activities for the Mandiant Consulting organization, including creating Statement Of Work (SOW) and other agreement documents. * Work within a case management system, ensure requests are progressing and regular timely updates are made. Drive end to end resolution, including engaging with other departments as required. * Create and drive to completion solutions to address non\-standard customer situations and document approaches for re\-use. * Assist with governance related activities that help the consulting organization become more efficient. * Assist with gathering information for customer on\-boarding requirements, working with business and consultants to resolve pricing/scoping issues, and liaising with customers and partners in discussions. title: PET CT Clinical Specialist

This role involves a combination of expert clinical duties, such as ensuring the correct protocols are followed and manage the day\-to\-day operations of the PET\-CT service, and leadership responsibilities, like training staff and supervising service delivery. **Key Responsibilities:** * In conjunction with the PET\-CT Clinical and Administrative staff, provide a safe \& efficient PET\-CT service, while adhering to all legislative regulations and company policies. * Accountable for the quality of the service provided, including customer service \& technical aspects. * Developing and implementing operational policies, protocols \& guidelines. * Maintain up to date knowledge of clinical, technical and radiographic developments in relation to the PET\-CT department and promote awareness of new developments. * Support the Unit Manager in the implementation of initiatives aimed at on\-going service development and improvement. * Promote \& maintain a safe environment for staff and patients. Assist in the development and implementation of risk management and health and safety strategies in association with the Quality Department. * Participate fully with all internal and external audits and Accreditation. * Quality Assurance: Investigate \& take appropriate action in accordance with Company policy in relation to complaints, accidents and incidents. * Ensure that all Company policies \& relevant up\-to\-date information is understood by staff and complied with. * Ensure all Company policies on infection control, IV policy and health and safety etc. are understood by staff and complied with. * Maintain a quality assurance programme in the PET/CT department. * Assist the Radiation Protection Officer to ensure that all principles and policies of radiation protection, are adhered to. * Be familiar with all legislation and directives regarding the safety standards for the protection of health workers and the general public against the dangers arising from Ionising Radiation. * Follow all Radiation Protection practices as determined by the Radiation Protection Committee, Radiation Protection Advisor and Radiation Protection Officer **Key Requirements:** * BSc (Hons) Diagnostic Radiography or equivalent * Active CORU Registration * IV Cannulation * Post\-Graduate Qualification in PET\-CT (desirable) but not mandatory. **Why choose Alliance Medical?** * Competitive Salary * Relocation Allowance (t\&c's) * Comprehensive Induction \& training programme * Family Friendly Working Arrangements * 27 Annual Leave Days (including 2 discretionary days) * Full paid private Health Insurance * Paid Maternity Leave * Wedding Day Leave * Paid Sick Leave * Fertility Leave * Excellent Pension Scheme * Educational Assistance * Wellbeing Initiatives * Excellent overtime rates \#AMDI

Our studio\-based approach to teaching and research creates the environment for critical enquiry, radical experimentation and creative learning, helping our students and staff to address many of the real challenges confronting society, culture and contemporary business.
